title: scry
description:
Searchable GraphQL ↔ MCP bridge for AI agents. Connects to one or many
GraphQL endpoints, introspects each schema, builds a per-upstream SQLite
+ FTS5 + BM25 index, and exposes ten+ MCP tools an agent can call.
v0.1 shipped core; v0.2 hardened deployment + audit + credential
handling for hosted fleets; v0.3 closes the audit trust loop and
adds smarter performance / change-detection knobs.

features.title: Audit chain anchor sidecar
features.description:
v0.2's rotation drops the prev-hash of the surviving chain head
when --audit-keep > 0, so VerifyChain trips at index 0 after a
truncation. Right fix: write a per-session anchor sidecar
(<session>.anchor) carrying the ChainHash of the last record in
the oldest still-retained archive. Verification reads the
anchor on load and uses it as the genesis prev-hash, so the
whole surviving chain validates end-to-end. Closes the
documented v0.2 trade-off in
TestAuditRotationKeepTruncatesAndDocumentsBoundary.

features.title: OTel logs bridge for audit chain
features.description:
Emit each Evidence record as an OTel log alongside the JSONL
file when OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER is set. Lets operators ship audit
to Splunk / Datadog / Loki / any OTLP-receiving SIEM without
scry-specific shipping code. JSONL stays as the durable
local-first record; OTel is the streaming sink.
Implementation: thin wrapper in obs/audit.go that constructs
an LogRecord per Evidence + emits via the configured logger
provider. Falls back to no-op when the exporter is unset.

features.title: Adopt mcp-go ToolFilter once PR
features.description:
mcp-go PR #92 introduces a first-party ToolFilter middleware.
When it merges + releases: bump the mcp-go dep, replace
internal/server/tool_filter.go with one-liner usage of
mcp.ToolFilter(predicate), delete the wrapper + its tests
(replaced by mcp-go's own coverage). If still pending at v0.3
freeze: leave internal wrapper, document the deferral.
Bonus: file ResourceFilter + PromptFilter follow-up PRs once
ToolFilter lands.

features.title: Schema diff alerting on refresh
features.description:
Today's background refresh swaps the cached schema silently —
operators only notice schema breaks when a cached query
suddenly fails validation. Detect changes: hash the SDL before
+ after refresh; on mismatch, compute a summary diff (added /
removed / changed types) via gqlparser, emit a structured log
event `schema.changed` + an OTel metric
`scry.schema.changes_total{kind=added|removed|breaking}`.
Operators wire that to their alerting (Datadog monitor,
Prometheus rule). New MCP tool `schema_diff(server, limit)`
surfaces the last N changes for agents that want to plan
around them.

features.title: TTL query result cache
features.description:
Many agents call the same query repeatedly during a task (re-
validating context). Add a per-server in-memory cache keyed by
SHA-256(query+variables+operationName), with TTL set via
--cache-ttl (default 30s, 0 disables). Mutations bypass
unconditionally (effect=write). Cache hits skip upstream +
record evidence with outcome=ok_cached so audit + metrics
distinguish cache vs upstream. Eviction: simple max-entries
LRU when --cache-max-entries is set; otherwise unbounded for
TTL semantics only.
